name: Pat Egan's Christmas Tree Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Green sugar crystals (about 1/4 cup Confetti or nonpareil candy)
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 20 minutes Chilling time: 4 hours Baking time: 10 minutes
1. Combine flour, sugar, butter, baking powder, salt, almond extract and egg in a large bowl of an electric mixer. Mix together using low speed. The dough will be crumbly. Knead the dough with hands until it holds together.
2. Remove 1/3 cup of the dough; wrap and refrigerate. Divide the remaining dough into thirds. Using hands, roll each portion into a 6-inch log.
3. Place sugar crystals on a sheet of wax paper. Roll each log in the sugar crystals to coat well, using the wax paper as a guide to press in crystals. Shape each log into a triangle, pressing gently on the wax paper to give three sharp corners. Ensure two sides are longer than the third side. Wrap each log tightly and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or until firm enough to slice.
4. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. To bake, slice logs crosswise into 1/4-inch slices. Place slices about 1 inch apart on ungreased cookie sheets. For each cookie, shape about 1/2 teaspoon of the reserved 1/3 cup dough into a tree trunk and attach to the bottom underside of each tree. Sprinkle each cookie lightly with candy decorations if desired. Bake until lightly browned, about 10 minutes. Remove to wire racks and allow to cool completely.
Note: The logs can be divided and shaped into three sizes and sliced to form a three-tiered tree. These second-place winning cookies are easily shaped into trees edged with glittery green sugar. Of her simple cookies, Pat Egan of Dolton, Illinois writes, "They're not a fancy or gourmet cookie, but a simple symbol of the Christmas season that is so close to my heart." from the Chicago Tribune annual Food Guide Holiday Cookie Contest December 8, 1988
